creator: vermaden@interia.pl
text:
Added mine test also below.

In short - That *mail -f* displays only ONE email while there are/should be TWO.




root@w520 # mail vermaden    
Subject: TEST1
asd
EOT



root@w520 # mail vermaden
Subject: TEST2
asd
EOT



vermaden@w520 % less ~/mbox
From root@w520.local Mon Sep 9 13:36:38 2024
Received: from root (uid 0)
 (envelope-from root@w520.local)
 id d03d2
 by w520.local (DragonFly Mail Agent v0.13+ on w520.local);
 Mon, 09 Sep 2024 13:36:38 +0200
To: vermaden
Subject: TEST2
Date: Mon, 09 Sep 2024 13:36:38 +0200
Message-Id: <66deddc6.d03d2.6b1acf51@w520.local>
From: <root@w520.local>
Status: RO

asd
From root@w520.local Mon Sep 9 13:36:33 2024
Received: from root (uid 0)
 (envelope-from root@w520.local)
 id d0454
 by w520.local (DragonFly Mail Agent v0.13+ on w520.local);
 Mon, 09 Sep 2024 13:36:33 +0200
To: vermaden
Subject: TEST1
Date: Mon, 09 Sep 2024 13:36:33 +0200
Message-Id: <66deddc1.d0454.6eb27e09@w520.local>
From: <root@w520.local>
Status: RO

asd



vermaden@w520 % mail -f ~/mbox 
Mail version 8.1 6/6/93. Type ? for help.
"/home/vermaden/mbox": 1 message
> 1 root@w520.local Mon Sep 9 13:36 29/731 
"TEST2"
& 



... or am I doing something wrong here?



Regards,
vermaden
